intent: Propose a falsifiable search-return question and controlled benchmarks for consumable certificate stock and depletion.

entity.title: Can certificate depletion make the return to inspecting an uncertified seller turn twice?
entity.summary: A proposal to test a strict reversal in inspection, successful allocation, or buyer surplus as certification cost changes the certificate stock and residual uncertified pool.

payload.phenomenon: A lower k changes entry into the stock of certificates, while buyer trade removes certificates selectively. These flows change the posterior quality of an uncertified seller and therefore the value of paying c for inspection.
payload.object_to_explain: Fix all primitives other than k and use one equilibrium-selection rule throughout. In the selected stationary symmetric Markov equilibrium, let r(k,s) be the buyer's expected gain from inspecting one uncertified seller rather than taking its best no-inspection action at public certificate state s, let I(k) be the stationary inspection rate, let A(k) be the probability the buyer trades with a high-capacity seller, and let U(k)=A(k)-cI(k). Determine whether there are 0<k1<k2<k3 such that [Y(k2)-Y(k1)][Y(k3)-Y(k2)]<0 for at least one Y in {I,A,U}, and whether the reversal changes when the stationary certificate-stock law, trade depletion, or the inspection margin is held fixed as declared below.
payload.unresolved_delta: It is unknown whether variation in r(k,s) induced by the stationary stock and the uncertified posterior is sufficient for the strict three-cost reversal, rather than the observation being generated by direct seller cost accounting or by equilibrium selection.
payload.importance: The answer determines whether cheaper public certification and costly private inspection are substitutes in a simple static sense or must be evaluated jointly with the inventory and depletion of public signals; this changes what a certification-cost intervention is required to track.
payload.kill_condition: Kill the proposed depletion-to-search mechanism if, under every admissible primitive vector and one fixed selection rule, the strict three-cost test fails for I, A, and U, or if any candidate reversal remains identical when the full stationary certificate-stock and uncertified-pool laws are replaced by their stock-free counterparts. An equilibrium-selection switch is an automatic kill for the claimed example.
payload.proposed_scope: The seed's two long-lived sellers, sequential short-lived buyers, privately observed high or low capacity, truthful high-capacity certification at cost k, one certificate that remains visible until trade consumes it, optional inspection of one uncertified seller at cost c, and stationary symmetric Markov strategies under one common selection rule. This proposal does not solve the game or rank policies.
prohibited_claims: No direction or existence of a reversal is asserted.
No proof, validation, uniqueness, or welfare result is asserted.
No benchmark is compared under a different equilibrium-selection rule.

entity.title: Benchmarks for the certificate-stock effect on inspection return
entity.summary: A full environment and four controlled counterfactuals separate inventory, trade depletion, direct cost accounting, and endogenous inspection.

benchmarks.label: Full consumable-certificate stock
exact_primitives: Two long-lived sellers face one new short-lived buyer per period.
Only a high-capacity seller may pay k for a truthful visible certificate.
An untraded certificate carries forward and a trade with its seller consumes it.
The buyer observes certificates, may inspect one uncertified seller at cost c, may buy without inspection, or may exit.
The capacity law, seller margin, seller discount factor, and equilibrium-selection rule are fixed across k.
timing: The buyer observes the updated public state and chooses inspection, purchase, or exit.
Service occurs and a certificate is removed exactly after trade with its seller.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Selected stationary symmetric Markov perfect equilibrium under one public selection rule common to all k.
benchmarks.prediction: Measure r(k,s), I(k), A(k), and U(k) and test the strict three-cost sign change; impose no direction.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: The full path combines direct cost, certification, inventory, depletion, composition, and inspection responses.

benchmarks.label: One-period certification with zero inherited stock
exact_primitives: All full-model primitives and the selection rule are unchanged.
A certificate expires after the current buyer whether or not its seller trades, so no certificate is inherited.
Certification and inspection remain endogenous within the period.
timing: Each period begins with zero certificate stock.
Certification, buyer observation, inspection, purchase or exit, and service follow the full-model order.
All certificates expire before the next period.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Selected stationary symmetric Markov perfect equilibrium under the full model's selection rule.
benchmarks.prediction: Full versus zero-stock isolates the role of inherited certificate inventory and its effect on the uncertified posterior; no sign is imposed.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Whether a strict reversal remains without inventory carryover.

benchmarks.label: Certificate stock without trade-triggered depletion
exact_primitives: All full-model primitives and the selection rule are unchanged.
A visible certificate carries forward after both trade and no trade.
Only the transition following a certified trade differs.
timing: All within-period actions follow the full model.
After trade, retain rather than consume the traded seller's certificate.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Selected stationary symmetric Markov perfect equilibrium under the full model's selection rule.
benchmarks.prediction: Full versus no-depletion isolates the trade-contingent certificate outflow while retaining carryover; no sign is imposed.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Whether the event-contingent depletion rule is necessary for the reversal.

benchmarks.label: Mechanical k change with histories frozen
exact_primitives: Freeze certification, inspection, purchase, and exit policies and the certificate-state distribution at their selected k0 values.
Change only the certification payment in seller payoff accounting from k0 to k.
timing: Generate exactly the same histories and actions at every counterfactual k.
Apply k only to the seller's certification expenditure.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Fixed-policy accounting counterfactual, not a re-solved equilibrium away from k0.
benchmarks.prediction: The buyer-side objects r, I, A, and U are invariant by construction; this benchmark records only the mechanical seller cost change.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Which re-equilibrated changes lie outside direct certification expenditure.

benchmarks.label: Full stock with the inspection map fixed
exact_primitives: Use the same predeclared reference cost k0.
Impose at every public state the selected k0 inspect-or-not rule for all k.
Re-solve certification, post-signal purchase or exit, certificate depletion, and the stationary state distribution at each k.
All other primitives and the selection rule match the full model.
timing: Stock and seller certification evolve at the current k.
The buyer follows the imposed k0 inspection map and then chooses purchase or exit.
Trade depletion and the stationary distribution adjust subject to that inspection constraint.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Constrained selected stationary symmetric Markov equilibrium with only the inspection map fixed.
benchmarks.prediction: Full versus inspection-fixed isolates adjustment of endogenous inspection while retaining a re-equilibrated certificate stock; no sign is imposed.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Whether the turning-point test survives when the inspection decision cannot adjust with k.

payload.exact_question_delta: Use common primitives and one selection rule. Compare full with zero-stock for inventory carryover, full with no-depletion for the trade-triggered outflow, re-equilibrated full with frozen histories for the direct cost-accounting term, and full with inspection-fixed for endogenous inspection adjustment. Attribute a reversal to the consumable-stock mechanism only if the strict three-cost criterion is met without an equilibrium-selection switch and changes under the declared stock or depletion benchmark.

outcome.rationale: The candidate proposes one strict search-return question and exact stock, depletion, accounting, and inspection comparisons without asserting a direction or result.
